release.action:
Machai Initial Release:

This initial release of Machai introduces a foundational toolkit designed to streamline developer automation with the power of Generative AI (GenAI). It establishes the core functionality and APIs required for researching and building modular workflows. Key highlights of this release include:

Core Features:
  • Unified GenAI Access: A provider-agnostic Java client that enables seamless interaction with multiple GenAI services, abstracting differences between providers for consistent usage.
  • Bindex Metadata Management: Tools for generating, aggregating, and registering Bindex metadata, empowering developers to discover and reuse libraries efficiently.
  • Semantic Project Assembly: Prompt-driven workflows that leverage Bindex metadata for intelligent library selection and automated project assembly based on semantic picking.
  • Automated Documentation with Ghostwriter: Repository-scale automation for documentation updates and transformations, guided by embedded GenAI-powered insights.

This release sets the foundation for exploring and expanding Machai’s capabilities, providing developers with essential tools to integrate GenAI into their workflows and enhance productivity through metadata-driven automation.

Machai Release Highlights:

This release of Machai builds upon the foundational functionality introduced in the initial release, delivering significant enhancements to developer automation capabilities, robustness, and usability. Key updates include:

Features
  • Expanded GenAI Provider Support: Added pluggable providers (OpenAI, Gemini, Claude, CodeMie) with safety checks and enhanced function-tool support for file, web, and command interactions.
  • Bindex Metadata Improvements: Enhanced library discovery workflows with updated picker selection, repository registration behavior, and improved assembly prompting.
  • Ghostwriter Automation Enhancements: Expanded documentation automation with file processors and language reviewers for Java, Markdown, HTML, Python, TypeScript, and PUML.
  • Act System Enhancements: Introduced reusable automation templates for tasks like unit tests, release notes, sonar fixes, and security issue resolution. Added support for custom act directories and URL-based act locations.
  • Maven Plugin Updates: Added reactor goals, project-layout detection for Maven/JS/Gradle-style projects, and enhanced integration with Ghostwriter and Acts.
  • Improved CLI Functionality: Enhanced CLI help output, act listing with descriptions, and command-line properties replacement support.
  • Unit Test Coverage Expansion: Extended test coverage across modules, including bindex-core builder/tool tests, project layout tests, OpenAI tool invocation tests, and additional CLI/assembly tests.
Fixes
  • Provider Robustness: Improved OpenAI provider handling for tool-call follow-ups, timeouts, and error recovery. Hardened command execution tools with denylist fixes and clearer error handling.
  • Act Processing Fixes: Addressed edge cases in act template loading, inheritance, placeholders, and non-interactive execution for CLI and Maven.
  • Security Updates: Resolved Grype-reported vulnerabilities (e.g., CVE-2025-48924, CVE-2021-26291) and updated dependency versions to eliminate security risks.
  • Command and API Improvements: Fixed callRestApi error handling, corrected command-line denylist validation, and improved non-recursive mode processing.
  • Maven Plugin and Assembly Fixes: Fixed reactor build scanning, assembly command logging, and PlantUML Maven plugin configuration.
Refactoring
  • Ghostwriter Pipeline Improvements: Streamlined shared module processing, normalized prompt/tool output, and improved deterministic template application.
  • Bindex and Act API Refinements: Simplified prompt/build flows, improved concurrency handling, and standardized project.dir usage across processors.
  • Code Cleanup: Removed unused dependencies, refined variable naming, and eliminated internal helper types for cleaner and more maintainable code.

This release significantly enhances Machai’s capabilities, delivering improved developer automation workflows, stronger security measures, and expanded support for GenAI providers.

Machai Release Highlights:

This release introduces significant enhancements to Machai, focusing on expanding functionality, improving robustness, and streamlining workflows for GenAI-enabled developer automation. Key updates include:

Features
  • Dedicated Embedding Provider Abstraction: Added support for separate embedding model configurations, enabling more flexible and modular embedding workflows.
  • Enhanced GenAI Provider Support: Expanded provider capabilities with Claude, Gemini, and CodeMie, including Beta API integration, web search functionality, and improved model routing.
  • Improved Functional Tooling: Introduced new tools for applying file patches, searching command logs, reading previous log chunks, and handling command-line properties replacements.
  • Act System Enhancements: Added reusable automation templates for tasks like unit tests, release notes, and sonar fixes. Acts now support URL-based locations and improved CLI help outputs.
  • Expanded Bindex Metadata Tooling: Enhanced library discovery workflows with updated picker selection, repository registration, and assembly prompting.
  • Ghostwriter Enhancements: Improved documentation automation with file processors and language reviewers for Java, Markdown, HTML, Python, TypeScript, and PUML.
  • Removed machai-cli Module: Consolidated functionality into more streamlined components.
Fixes
  • Improved Provider Robustness: Enhanced OpenAI provider handling for tool-call follow-ups, timeouts, and error recovery. Fixed cache token calculations and corrected tool limit constants.
  • Act and CLI Fixes: Addressed edge cases in act processing, template inheritance, placeholder handling, and non-interactive execution for CLI and Maven.
  • Security Updates: Resolved Grype-reported vulnerabilities (e.g., CVE-2025-48924) and updated dependencies to eliminate security risks.
  • Command Execution Fixes: Hardened command execution tools with denylist fixes and improved validation for command-line inputs.
  • Maven Plugin and Assembly Fixes: Fixed reactor build scanning, improved assembly command logging, and corrected PlantUML Maven plugin configurations.
Refactoring
  • Embedding and Provider Improvements: Refactored embedding support across OpenAI and CodeMie providers, simplifying APIs and improving maintainability.
  • Act and Bindex Refinements: Streamlined act workflows, prompt parsing, and concurrency handling. Updated APIs for clarity and usability.
  • Code Cleanup: Removed unused dependencies, refined variable naming, and eliminated internal helper types for cleaner and more maintainable code.

This release builds upon Machai's foundational capabilities, delivering improved functionality, greater flexibility, and enhanced developer automation workflows.
